channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-05-24T13:49:31.569Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with Africa CDC confirming active outbreak monitoring in both the DRC and Uganda. Multi-health-zone spread in eastern DRC continues; cross-border movement elevates geographic risk. Specific case and fatality counts require direct verification against the current WHO Disease Outbreak News (who.int/emergencies/disease-outbreak-news) or Africa CDC epidemic intelligence report.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 11 reported cases (9 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 1,952 measles cases across 40 jurisdictions in 2026 (as of May 21), representing 29 outbreaks with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: NBC News: DR Congo’s neighbors impose Covid-style measures in push to limit Ebola’s spread
item.description: Regional concern over the Ebola outbreak intensified Saturday as Uganda confirmed three new cases, while neighboring countries stepped up monitoring measures amid fears of cross-border spread.

item.title: Google News: DR Congo’s neighbors impose Covid-style measures in push to limit Ebola’s spread - NBC News
item.description: DR Congo’s neighbors impose Covid-style measures in push to limit Ebola’s spread NBC News

item.title: NPR: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring
item.description: The number of cases — and deaths — in Bangladesh is staggering. As of Sunday, 528 have died, mostly children. How did this measles outbreak begin? And how is the country responding?

item.title: Google News: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring - NPR
item.description: More than 500 children have died in an outbreak that the world is virtually ignoring NPR

item.title: Google News: Nature Comms: Dairy cows infected with influenza A(H5N1) reveals low infectious dose and transmission barriers - Avian Flu Diary
item.description: Nature Comms: Dairy cows infected with influenza A(H5N1) reveals low infectious dose and transmission barriers Avian Flu Diary

item.title: NPR: Mind-bending photos by anonymous cousins show the pain and dreams of Afghan women
item.description: The young women make photos that look at life — how it is, how they wish it could be — under Taliban rule. The images are on display at the Photoville Festival in Brooklyn, New York.

item.title: NBC News: Toxic Chemical Tank Threat Forces Evacuations in California
item.description: A huge industrial tank filled with a toxic chemical is at risk of exploding in Orange County, California, and efforts to cool the tank and buy more time are failing. The situation has put 40,000 thousand people under mandatory evacuation orders and Gov. Gavin Newsom declared...

item.title: Google News: 16 children die from measles-like symptoms in 24 hours across Bangladesh - newagebd.net
item.description: 16 children die from measles-like symptoms in 24 hours across Bangladesh newagebd.net

item.description: Rush for new Ebola vaccine as outbreak spreads DW.com

item.title: NPR: Nurse convicted in patient's death is now a national speaker on hospital safety
item.description: RaDonda Vaught was convicted of negligent homicide after dispensing the wrong drug to a patient. She now gives speeches about hospital safety in an era of automation and artificial intelligence.

item.title: Google News: Africa on edge as Ebola outbreak threatens 10 countries and triggers global alarm - Business Insider Africa
item.description: Africa on edge as Ebola outbreak threatens 10 countries and triggers global alarm Business Insider Africa
